beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check ferry schedule and weather
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning routine; Diego is routine-bound and weather-aware, so he'd check conditions early. The calm sunny forecast means the ferry will be pleasant.

beats.activity: Walk to Mercer Island ferry terminal
beats.reasoning: Sunday is a light work day for the florist stall, but Diego maintains his routine and heads in early to prep. Ferries run less frequently on Sunday, so he's careful with timing.
Calm water and sunny conditions make this a pleasant crossing. Diego obsessively tracks the ferry schedule and takes it daily—this is his preferred commute.

beats.activity: Walk from ferry dock to Pike Place Market stall
beats.reasoning: Short walk from the ferry terminal through downtown to his florist stall. Sunday mornings are quieter, so he can prep the stall without the Thursday rush.

beats.activity: Work at florist stall: arrange flowers, interact with customers
beats.reasoning: Sunday is a lighter market day but still brings weekend tourists and locals. Diego's stall runs shorter hours on Sunday; he'll close by early afternoon to enjoy the good weather.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby market vendor
beats.reasoning: Diego eats standing up at the market like always on work days. Quick sandwich or similar before heading out to enjoy the afternoon sunshine.

beats.activity: Walk from Pike Place to Lake Union waterfront; Sit at Lake Union, watch boats and paddlers, relax
beats.reasoning: The forecast says heavy recreational boat traffic in the afternoon and high of 72F—perfect weather. Diego values quiet time and would head to the water to watch the boats and enjoy the calm Sunday vibe.
Time to head home before the evening rush. Diego is methodical about ferry timing and will catch a late-afternoon sailing.

beats.activity: Ferry ride home across Lake Union
beats.reasoning: Evening light on calm water; another contemplative ferry ride. Diego savors these moments.

beats.activity: Walk from ferry terminal to home
beats.reasoning: Final walk of the day as daylight lingers on this June evening.

diary:
Sunday felt like a gift. Woke early to perfect weather—coffee tasted better knowing the ferry wouldn't be packed. The crossing was smooth, water glassy. Pike Place was busy but manageable; sold out of the white roses by noon, which always makes me smile. A young couple came in looking for an anniversary arrangement, and I remembered why I do this.

After lunch, I ditched the stall early and walked down to Lake Union. Sat on a bench for three hours just watching the boats—kayaks, sailboats, paddleboarders everywhere. The sun was warm on my shoulders. Didn't think about much of anything. Just breathed. Ferry ride home felt peaceful, Mercer Island quiet as always. These days are rarer than they should be.
thought_process: I've learned to recognize when the city is offering me something. Seventy-two degrees and no chaos meant I could actually be present with customers instead of rushing through the motions. The lunch break turned into something longer—I caught myself wanting to stay put rather than hurry back. That's when I knew I needed the waterfront. Work has been steady but grinding lately, and I think I needed permission to just sit. The boat traffic was almost meditative to watch. Ferry rides are my thinking time anyway, but today I didn't want to think. Just be. The quiet Sundays keep me sane.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
